78			Short: "Run specs",
79			Long:  `Run specs.`,
80			Example: `  gauge run specs/
81	  gauge run --tags "login" -s -p specs/`,
82			Run: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
83				logger.Debugf(true, "gauge %s %v", cmd.Name(), strings.Join(args, " "))
84				if err := config.SetProjectRoot(args); err != nil {
85					exit(err, "")
86				}
87				if er := handleConflictingParams(cmd.Flags(), args); er != nil {
88					exit(er, "")
89				}
90				if repeat {
91					repeatLastExecution(cmd)
92				} else if failed {
93					executeFailed(cmd)
94				} else {
95					addFlagsToExecutionArgs(cmd.Flags())
96					execute(cmd, args)
97				}
98			},
99			DisableAutoGenTag: true,

124	func init() {
125		GaugeCmd.AddCommand(runCmd)
126		f := runCmd.Flags()

129		f.StringVarP(&environment, environmentName, "e", environmentDefault, "Specifies the environment to use")
130		f.StringVarP(&tags, tagsName, "t", tagsDefault, "Executes the specs and scenarios tagged with given tags")
131		f.StringVarP(&rows, rowsName, "r", rowsDefault, "Executes the specs and scenarios only for the selected rows. It can be specified by range as 2-4 or as list 2,4")
132		f.BoolVarP(&parallel, parallelName, "p", parallelDefault, "Execute specs in parallel")
133		f.IntVarP(&streams, streamsName, "n", streamsDefault, "Specify number of parallel execution streams")
134		f.IntVarP(&maxRetriesCount, maxRetriesCountName, "c", maxRetriesCountDefault, "Max count of iterations for failed scenario")
135		f.StringVarP(&retryOnlyTags, retryOnlyTagsName, "", retryOnlyTagsDefault, "Retries the specs and scenarios tagged with given tags")
136		f.StringVarP(&tagsToFilterForParallelRun, onlyName, "o", onlyDefault, "Execute only the specs and scenarios tagged with given tags in parallel, rest will be run in serial. Applicable only if run in parallel.")
137		err := f.MarkHidden(onlyName)
138		if err != nil {
139			logger.Errorf(false, "Unable to mark '%s' flag as hidden: %s", onlyName, err.Error())
140		}
141		f.IntVarP(&group, groupName, "g", groupDefault, "Specify which group of specification to execute based on -n flag")
142		f.StringVarP(&strategy, strategyName, "", strategyDefault, "Set the parallelization strategy for execution. Possible options are: `eager`, `lazy`")
143		f.StringVarP(&sort, sortName, "s", sortDefault, "Set the order of spec execution. Possible options are: `alpha`, `random`")
144		// Set NoOptDefVal to "alpha" for backward compatibility: -s without value = alphabetical sort
145		f.Lookup(sortName).NoOptDefVal = "alpha"
146		f.Int64Var(&randomSeed, randomSeedName, randomSeedDefault, "Random seed for reproducible random execution. Used only when --sort=random")
147		f.BoolVarP(&installPlugins, installPluginsName, "i", installPluginsDefault, "Install All Missing Plugins")
148		f.BoolVarP(&failed, failedName, "f", failedDefault, "Run only the scenarios failed in previous run. This cannot be used in conjunction with any other argument")
149		f.BoolVarP(&repeat, repeatName, "", repeatDefault, "Repeat last run. This cannot be used in conjunction with any other argument")
150		f.BoolVarP(&hideSuggestion, hideSuggestionName, "", hideSuggestionDefault, "Hide step implementation stub for every unimplemented step")
151		f.BoolVarP(&failSafe, failSafeName, "", failSafeDefault, "Force return 0 exit code, even in case of failures.")
152		f.BoolVarP(&skipCommandSave, skipCommandSaveName, "", skipCommandSaveDefault, "Skip saving last command in lastRunCmd.json")
153		err = f.MarkHidden(skipCommandSaveName)
154		if err != nil {
155			logger.Errorf(false, "Unable to mark '%s' flag as hidden: %s", skipCommandSaveName, err.Error())
156		}
157	
158		f.StringArrayVar(&scenarios, scenarioName, scenarioNameDefault, "Set scenarios for running specs with scenario name")
159	}

161	func executeFailed(cmd *cobra.Command) {
162		lastState, err := rerun.GetLastFailedState()
163		if err != nil {
164			exit(err, "")
165		}
166		if !skipCommandSave {
167			rerun.WritePrevArgs(os.Args)
168		}
169		handleFlags(cmd, append([]string{"gauge"}, lastState...))
170		err = cmd.Flags().Set(skipCommandSaveName, "true")
171		if err != nil {
172			logger.Errorf(false, "Unable to set '%s' flag as 'true': %s", skipCommandSaveName, err.Error())
173		}
174		logger.Debugf(true, "Executing => %s\n", strings.Join(os.Args, " "))
175		err = cmd.Execute()
176		if err != nil {
177			logger.Errorf(true, "Unable to execute command %s: %s", cmd.Name(), err.Error())
178		}
179	}
180	
181	func handleFlags(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
182		cmd.Flags().Visit(func(flag *pflag.Flag) {
183			if !util.ListContains(overrideRerunFlags, flag.Name) && flag.Changed {
184				err := flag.Value.Set(flag.DefValue)
185				if err != nil {
186					logger.Errorf(false, "Unable to set default value in '%s' flag: %s", flag.Name, err.Error())
187				}
188			}
189		})
190	
191		for i := 0; i <= len(args)-1; i++ {
192			f := lookupFlagFromArgs(cmd, args[i])
193			if f == nil {
194				continue
195			}
196			v := f.Value.String()
197			_, err := strconv.ParseBool(v)
198			if err != nil && f.Changed {
199				if strings.Contains(args[i], "=") {
200					args[i] = strings.SplitAfter(args[i], "=")[0] + f.Value.String()
201				} else {
202					args[i+1] = v
203					i = i + 1
204				}
205			}
206		}
207		os.Args = args
208	}
209	
210	func lookupFlagFromArgs(cmd *cobra.Command, arg string) *pflag.Flag {
211		fName := strings.Split(strings.TrimLeft(arg, "-"), "=")[0]
212		flags := cmd.Flags()
213		f := flags.Lookup(fName)
214		if f == nil && len(fName) == 1 {
215			f = flags.ShorthandLookup(fName)
216		}
217		return f
218	}
219	
220	func addFlagsToExecutionArgs(flags *pflag.FlagSet) {
221		flags.Visit(func(flag *pflag.Flag) {
222			execution.ExecutionArgs = append(execution.ExecutionArgs, &gauge.ExecutionArg{
223				Name:  flag.Name,
224				Value: []string{flag.Value.String()},
225			})
226		})
227	}
228	
229	func installMissingPlugins(flag, languageOnly bool) {
230		if flag && os.Getenv("GAUGE_PLUGIN_INSTALL") != "false" {
231			install.AllPlugins(machineReadable, languageOnly)
232		}
233	}
234	
235	func execute(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
236		for _, arg := range execution.ExecutionArgs {
237			logger.Debugf(true, "%s : %v", arg.Name, arg.Value)
238		}
239		loadEnvAndReinitLogger(cmd)
240		ensureScreenshotsDir()
241		if parallel && tagsToFilterForParallelRun != "" && !env.AllowFilteredParallelExecution() {
242			logger.Fatal(true, "Filtered parallel execution is a experimental feature. It can be enabled via allow_filtered_parallel_execution property.")
243		}
244		specs := getSpecsDir(args)
245		rerun.SaveState(os.Args[1:], specs)
246	
247		// Save command args with auto-generated seed if needed
248		cmdArgsToSave := os.Args
249		if sort == "random" && randomSeed == 0 {
250			// Generate seed now so it can be saved for reruns
251			randomSeed = time.Now().UnixNano()
252			order.RandomSeed = randomSeed
253			// Append the seed to the command args for saving
254			cmdArgsToSave = append(os.Args, fmt.Sprintf("--%s=%d", randomSeedName, randomSeed))
255		}
256	
257		if !skipCommandSave {
258			rerun.WritePrevArgs(cmdArgsToSave)
259		}
260		installMissingPlugins(installPlugins, false)
261		exitCode := execution.ExecuteSpecs(specs)
262		if failSafe && exitCode != execution.ParseFailed {
263			exitCode = 0
264		}
265		os.Exit(exitCode)
266	}
267	
268	var repeatLastExecution = func(cmd *cobra.Command) {
269		lastState := rerun.ReadPrevArgs()
270		handleFlags(cmd, lastState)
271		logger.Debugf(true, "Executing => %s\n", strings.Join(lastState, " "))
272		err := cmd.Execute()
273		if err != nil {
274			logger.Errorf(true, "Unable to execute command %s: %s", cmd.Name(), err.Error())
275		}
276	}
277	
278	func handleConflictingParams(setFlags *pflag.FlagSet, args []string) error {
279		flagDiffCount := 0
280		setFlags.Visit(func(flag *pflag.Flag) {
281			if !util.ListContains(overrideRerunFlags, flag.Name) && flag.DefValue != flag.Value.String() {
282				flagDiffCount++
283			}
284		})
285		if repeat && len(args)+flagDiffCount > 1 {
286			return errors.New("Invalid Command. Usage: gauge run --repeat")
287		}
288		if failed && len(args)+flagDiffCount > 1 {
289			return errors.New("Invalid Command. Usage: gauge run --failed")
290		}
291		if !parallel && tagsToFilterForParallelRun != "" {
292			return errors.New("Invalid Command. flag --only can be used only with --parallel")
293		}
294		if maxRetriesCount == 1 && retryOnlyTags != "" {
295			return errors.New("Invalid Command. flag --retry-only can be used only with --max-retry-count")
296		}
297		return nil
298	}
299

/*----------------------------------------------------------------

 *----------------------------------------------------------------*/

package rerun

import (
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"

	"github.com/getgauge/common"
	"github.com/getgauge/gauge-proto/go/gauge_messages"
	"github.com/getgauge/gauge/config"
	"github.com/getgauge/gauge/execution/result"
	"github.com/getgauge/gauge/gauge"
	"github.com/getgauge/gauge/util"

	"sort"

	. "gopkg.in/check.v1"
)

func Test(t *testing.T) { TestingT(t) }

type MySuite struct{}

var _ = Suite(&MySuite{})

func (s *MySuite) SetUpTest(c *C) {
	p, _ := filepath.Abs("_testdata")
	config.ProjectRoot = p
	failedMeta = newFailedMetaData()
}

func (s *MySuite) TestIfFailedFileIsCreated(c *C) {
	msg := "hello world"

	writeFailedMeta(msg)

	file := filepath.Join(config.ProjectRoot, common.DotGauge, failedFile)
	c.Assert(common.FileExists(file), Equals, true)
	expected := msg

	content, _ := os.ReadFile(file)

	c.Assert(string(content), Equals, expected)
	_ = os.RemoveAll(filepath.Join(config.ProjectRoot, common.DotGauge))
}

func (s *MySuite) TestGetScenarioFailedMetadata(c *C) {
	spec1Rel := filepath.Join("specs", "example1.spec")
	spec1Abs := filepath.Join(config.ProjectRoot, spec1Rel)
	sce := &gauge.Scenario{Span: &gauge.Span{Start: 2}}
	sr1 := &result.ScenarioResult{ProtoScenario: &gauge_messages.ProtoScenario{ExecutionStatus: gauge_messages.ExecutionStatus_FAILED}}

	prepareScenarioFailedMetadata(sr1, sce, &gauge_messages.ExecutionInfo{CurrentSpec: &gauge_messages.SpecInfo{FileName: spec1Abs}})

	c.Assert(len(failedMeta.failedItemsMap[spec1Abs]), Equals, 1)
	c.Assert(failedMeta.failedItemsMap[spec1Abs][spec1Rel+":2"], Equals, true)
}

func (s *MySuite) TestAddSpecPreHookFailedMetadata(c *C) {
	spec1Rel := filepath.Join("specs", "example1.spec")
	spec1Abs := filepath.Join(config.ProjectRoot, spec1Rel)
	spec1 := &result.SpecResult{ProtoSpec: &gauge_messages.ProtoSpec{PreHookFailures: []*gauge_messages.ProtoHookFailure{{ErrorMessage: "error"}}, FileName: spec1Abs}}

	addFailedMetadata(spec1, []string{}, addSpecFailedMetadata)

	c.Assert(len(failedMeta.failedItemsMap[spec1Rel]), Equals, 1)
	c.Assert(failedMeta.failedItemsMap[spec1Rel][spec1Rel], Equals, true)
}

func (s *MySuite) TestAddSpecPostHookFailedMetadata(c *C) {
	spec1Rel := filepath.Join("specs", "example1.spec")
	spec1Abs := filepath.Join(config.ProjectRoot, spec1Rel)
	spec1 := &result.SpecResult{ProtoSpec: &gauge_messages.ProtoSpec{PostHookFailures: []*gauge_messages.ProtoHookFailure{{ErrorMessage: "error"}}, FileName: spec1Abs}}

183			nFailedScenarios += specResult.ScenarioFailedCount
184			nSkippedScenarios += specResult.ScenarioSkippedCount
185		}
186		nExecutedScenarios -= nSkippedScenarios
187		nPassedScenarios = nExecutedScenarios - nFailedScenarios
188		if nExecutedScenarios < 0 {

Now I have a clear understanding of the bug. Let me trace the issue:

1. In `specExecutor.executeScenario()` (line 290-339), when a scenario is retried with `--max-retries-count`, it can eventually pass.
2. In `rerun.go`, the `prepareScenarioFailedMetadata` function (line 99-105) is called when `event.ScenarioEnd` fires. It checks `res.GetFailed()` (line 100) to determine if a scenario failed.
3. The issue is: when a scenario is retried with `--max-retries-count`, the `ScenarioEnd` event fires for EACH retry attempt (including the failed ones), so failed attempts of scenarios that eventually pass are still recorded as failed items.

Wait, let me re-check. Looking more carefully:

In `specExecutor.executeScenario()`, the `ScenarioEnd` event is fired from within the `scenarioExecutor.execute()` method (line 67: `defer event.Notify(...)`). So for each retry iteration, the scenario executor is called and the `ScenarioEnd` event fires for each retry. When a scenario fails on the first try and passes on the second, two `ScenarioEnd` events are fired: one for the failed attempt and one for the passed attempt.

But wait, looking at `prepareScenarioFailedMetadata`, it checks `res.GetFailed()`. Let me look more carefully at what `GetFailed()` returns for a passed scenario...

Actually, let me re-read the code flow more carefully. The key issue is: in `specExecutor.executeScenario()`, each retry creates a NEW `ScenarioResult` (line 318-323) for each retry iteration. The `ScenarioEnd` event fires for each one. So even when a scenario passes on retry, the previous failed attempt's `ScenarioEnd` event will have `GetFailed() == true`.

But wait, there might be a different problem. Let me look at this again. On each retry, a new `ScenarioResult` is created. The scenario executor sets `ProtoScenario.ExecutionStatus = gauge_messages.ExecutionStatus_PASSED` initially (line 49), then calls `scenarioResult.SetFailure()` on failure. So each retry's result object correctly reflects whether THAT specific attempt passed or failed.

Actually, looking at the code again, the issue might be simpler. Let me look at the `Event.Notify` for `ScenarioEnd`:

In `scenarioExecutor.execute()` line 67:
```go
defer event.Notify(event.NewExecutionEvent(event.ScenarioEnd, scenario, scenarioResult, e.stream, e.currentExecutionInfo))
```

So for each retry, when the scenario is re-executed:
1. First attempt fails → `ScenarioEnd` event with failed result → `prepareScenarioFailedMetadata` adds it as a failed item
2. Second attempt passes → `ScenarioEnd` event with passed result → `prepareScenarioFailedMetadata` does NOT add it

So from this analysis, the scenario should NOT be recorded as failed on the second attempt. But it's already been recorded as failed on the first attempt. The question is: does the code ever CLEAR a failed item from the map when a subsequent retry passes?

Looking at `prepareScenarioFailedMetadata` again:

```go
func prepareScenarioFailedMetadata(res *result.ScenarioResult, sce *gauge.Scenario, executionInfo *gauge_messages.ExecutionInfo) {
    if res.GetFailed() {
        specPath := executionInfo.GetCurrentSpec().GetFileName()
        failedScenario := util.RelPathToProjectRoot(specPath)
        failedMeta.addFailedItem(specPath, fmt.Sprintf("%s:%v", failedScenario, sce.Span.Start))
    }
}
```

This only ADDS on failure. It never REMOVES on subsequent pass. So if a scenario fails on retry 1 and passes on retry 2:
1. Retry 1: adds the scenario as failed 
2. Retry 2: the scenario passes, but the failed item is never removed

This is the bug! The `prepareScenarioFailedMetadata` function should REMOVE a scenario from the failed items map when it passes (after a previous retry), not just skip adding it.

Now let me also check the `ScenarioEnd` event to understand what's happening. Let me also look at how the event and `currentExecutionInfo` are structured for retries.

Let me also check if the `CurrentScenario.Retries` info in `currentExecutionInfo` tells us about retries, because we could potentially use that to distinguish original failures from retry passes.

Actually, the simplest fix would be: in `prepareScenarioFailedMetadata`, when a scenario PASSES, remove it from the `failedItemsMap` (for the spec path). Since the scenario may have previously been added as failed during an earlier retry, we need to clear it on success.

Let me verify my understanding by looking more closely at the issue:

In the loop in `executeScenario` (lines 307-336):
- For each retry, we create a NEW `ScenarioResult` 
- We call `e.scenarioExecutor.execute(scenario, scenarioResult)` which fires `ScenarioEnd`
- The `ScenarioEnd` event carries this new `ScenarioResult`

So:
1. Retry 0 (first attempt): scenario fails → `ScenarioEnd` fires with failed result → `prepareScenarioFailedMetadata` adds it
2. Retry 1 (second attempt): scenario passes → `ScenarioEnd` fires with passed result → `prepareScenarioFailedMetadata` does nothing (condition is `res.GetFailed()`)

The scenario is already in the failed items map from step 1. It never gets removed.

The fix should be to ALSO remove the item from the failed items map when a scenario passes on a retry (to handle the case where it was previously recorded as failed).

The bug: In `prepareScenarioFailedMetadata()` in `rerun.go`, when a scenario is retried with `--max-retries-count`, each retry fires a `ScenarioEnd` event. The bug is:

The diff shows exactly the intended change - only the `prepareScenarioFailedMetadata` function in `rerun.go` was modified, and no other files were changed.

Here's the summary of the bug and the fix:

**Bug:** When `--max-retries-count` is used and a scenario fails on a retry attempt but eventually passes, the `ScenarioEnd` event fires for each retry attempt. The first (failed) attempt triggers `prepareScenarioFailedMetadata` which adds the scenario to the `failedItemsMap`. When a subsequent retry passes, the scenario is never removed from the map. This means that when `gauge run --failed` is later executed, scenarios that actually passed after retry are still re-run as if they completely failed.

**Fix:** In the `prepareScenarioFailedMetadata` function, when a scenario result is NOT failed (i.e., it passed), we now remove it from the `failedItemsMap`. This handles the case where a previous retry attempt of the same scenario had been recorded as failed:
1. When a scenario fails → add it to `failedItemsMap` (existing behavior)
2. When a scenario passes → remove it from `failedItemsMap` (new behavior to fix the bug)
3. When the map entry for a spec becomes empty after removal → clean up the spec entry from the outer map
